plan: Queue jungle as primary. It is your best role at 51.5% over 154 games (your overall is 50.5%). Set support as secondary, not the other way around.
Stop queueing offlane: 41.5% over 135 games is costing real VP. When auto-filled there, play your safest comfort pick, not a learning pick.
Comfort-hero rule for the climb: Zinx. Compared with the average player on the same hero, you win about 2.5 more games per 100 on it.
Your champion pool is 11+ heroes wide and your top three are only 47% of your games. Gold-to-Platinum climbs are almost always pool-narrowing stories: aim for 70%+ of games on your top three.
The math: you are at 622 VP; Platinum III starts at 900. Your all-time peak is 903 — you have already touched Platinum-level rating once; this is a consistency problem, not a ceiling problem.
honesty: personal winrates are shrunk toward your own average (small heater samples do not count as mains)
field baselines are all-ranks aggregates from our current-patch match sample, not Gold-bracket-specific yet
winrate edges are observational; they say where you win, not why

insights.title: You are hotter than your career says
insights.finding: The last 20 games are 65% against a 51% career. Whatever changed recently, keep it.
insights.receipt: 13/20 recent wins vs 50.5% over 1140 career games

insights.title: You win the long game
insights.finding: Past 32 minutes you win 78% vs 63% in short games. Scaling comps and patience suit you; avoid coin-flip early all-ins.
insights.receipt: wins 5 of 8 short games vs 7 of 9 long ones, recent sample

insights.title: Gadget is the long-term relationship
insights.finding: 220 games and you genuinely out-pilot the field on Gadget — about 2.0 more wins per 100 games than the average Gadget player. The hours show.

insights.title: Mr_Meat31 brings out your best
insights.finding: Your record alongside Mr_Meat31 is 54% over 291 ranked games — your strongest pairing in the stack. When only two of you queue, make it this one.

insights.title: For the group chat
insights.finding: 1 pentakill in real 5v5s and a longest spree of 18 (plus 1 more in ARAM and brawls — the group chat can rule on whether those count). Willy has receipts.

coachReasoning.assessment: You are a steady 50.5% grinder sitting at Gold III, and you have already touched Platinum-level rating (peak 903 VP) before — so the single biggest lever is not skill, it is where your games go: stop bleeding them in offlane and pour volume into jungle and Zinx.
plan: Make jungle your primary queue and play it on a scaling tempo: clear, then look for picks once your hero comes online rather than coin-flip early dives — your record past 32 minutes is 78% vs 63% in short games, so let the game grow.
Drill Zinx as your climb pick: rush toward the Timewarp spike around minute 14 and learn to time your heal/shield output for the 20-second fights — that is exactly where Vesh adds value, so take it.
Stop queueing offlane entirely; move those games to jungle. When you are auto-filled to offlane, lock your safest comfort hero and play to scale, never a fresh learning pick.

Keep Gadget as your long-term main and lean on him in close games — you out-pilot the average Gadget player here, so trust the matchup knowledge you have built over 220 games rather than forcing a flashier pick.

Narrow your pool toward your strongest scaling heroes (Zinx, Gadget) so your top three carry more of your games; a wider pool is splitting your reps right when you need consistency to hold Platinum rating.

insights.title: The offlane is the leak
insights.finding: Offlane is 41.5% over 135 games while jungle is 53.9% over 154 — simply moving those offlane games to jungle is worth about 3.3 more wins per 100 games shifted.

insights.title: Ride the Zinx spike
insights.finding: Zinx is 57.3% over 164 games and is sim-optimal for heal/shield output, with its first spike landing around minute 14 on Timewarp — group up and fight once that spike is online.

insights.title: You win the long game
insights.finding: You win 78% of games past 32 minutes versus 63% in short ones — pick scaling comps and avoid early all-ins, because patience is your edge.

insights.title: You are trending up
insights.finding: Your last 20 games are 65% against a 51% career, so whatever changed recently is working — keep the jungle-and-Zinx pattern that got you hot.
